name: Virgen de la Peña Trail
name_original: Ermita de la Virgen de la Peña desde Puebla de Guzmán
description: Hike the moderate 7.3-mile Virgen de la Peña Trail near Puebla de Guzmán, leading to the historic Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña.
region_intro: Hiking around Puebla De Guzmán offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, areas influenced by historical mining, and proximity to bodies of water. The region features moderate elevation changes, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often traverse open countryside and can include sections near lakes or former industrial sites. Best hiking trails around Puebla De Guzmán The most popular hiking route is Virgen de la Peña Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain and views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rest area by the lake – Church of Mina de São Domingos loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.4 km) path. This trail explores the area around a lake and a historic mining church. Local hikers also love the Mina de São Domingos – Copper lakes of the mine loop from Santana de Cambas, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through former mining landscapes and past copper lakes, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Puebla De Guzmán is defined by rolling hills, historical mining areas, and lakeside paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Puebla De Guzmán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Puebla De Guzmán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Puebla De Guzmán?
A: Puebla De Guzmán offers a selection of over 15 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and skill levels. The region is known for its diverse landscape, including rolling hills and areas influenced by historical mining.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Puebla De Guzmán?
A: Yes, Puebla De Guzmán has 4 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapada Grande Bar – Rest area by the lake loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 3 miles (5 km) long.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Puebla De Guzmán?
A: The terrain around Puebla De Guzmán is characterized by rolling hills, areas influenced by historical mining, and paths near bodies of water. You'll find varied landscapes, from open countryside to trails exploring former industrial sites and lakeside areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Puebla De Guzmán?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Puebla De Guzmán are circular, offering convenient loop options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27625975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake – Church of Mina de São Domingos loop from Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop that explores the area around a lake and a historic mining church.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore several interesting sites. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7785262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Copper lakes of the mine&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2814217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina de São Domingos&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5333422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of La Peña of Puebla de Guzmán&lt;/a&gt;. These attractions offer a glimpse into the region's natural beauty and industrial past.

Q: Are there any longer distance trails for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are options for longer walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33805244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina de São Domingos – Copper lakes of the mine loop from Santana de Cambas&lt;/a&gt; is a 10-mile (16.1 km) trail that takes approximately 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, leading through former mining landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Puebla De Guzmán?
A: The routes in Puebla De Guzmán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain and the unique blend of natural and historical sights.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good viewpoints?
A: Given the region's rolling hills and varied landscape, many trails offer scenic views of the surrounding countryside. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984146197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de la Peña Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is known for its varied terrain and views, providing opportunities to appreciate the area's natural beauty.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Many trailheads, especially those near popular starting points like the Parque Estacionamento da Praia fluvial da Tapada Grande, offer designated parking areas. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before you go.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by lakes or rivers?
A: Yes, the region's proximity to bodies of water means you can find trails that traverse open countryside and include sections near lakes or rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21845762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest area by the lake – Mina de São Domingos loop&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that explores the area around a lake.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Puebla De Guzmán?
A: Puebla De Guzmán generally experiences mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. However, with proper preparation, hiking can be enjoyed year-round.
tips: It was built during the 16th century inside the Alfayat castle. It had a magnificent wooden coffered ceiling that rested on a double row of strong marble columns. It had a superb chestnut choir carved with two bells and closed by an old wrought iron gate, as is its pulpit.

The Hermitage is located on the so-called Cerro del Águila, about four kilometers from Puebla de Guzmán. It was probably built in the 16th century, although the most important construction works were undertaken in the 17th century.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for hiking this trail?
faqs.answer: The Virgen de la Peña Trail is a moderate hike that typically takes around 3 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This duration allows for enjoying the scenery and visiting the highlights along the way.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, with its moderate difficulty rating and an elevation gain of approximately 227 meters over nearly 12 kilometers, this trail is generally suitable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. It involves a steady climb but is often described as a wider path or road, making it accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Virgen de la Peña Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail involves a steady climb with an average gradient of 4.4%. Research suggests it might be a wider path or road, making it suitable for both hiking and biking. Expect a mix of natural paths and potentially some wider, more established sections.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary points of interest along the route are religious buildings. You will encounter the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Rock and the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña. These offer cultural significance and often provide scenic views from their elevated positions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the trailhead in Puebla de Guzmán are not explicitly provided, trails of this nature often have designated parking areas near the starting point, especially if they are also used by cyclists. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival in Puebla de Guzmán for the most convenient parking.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen de la Peña Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information specific to dogs on this particular trail in Puebla de Guzmán is not readily available. However, many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if they are wider paths. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Andalucía, Spain, the best times to hike would generally be during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). Summers can be very hot, making hiking less comfortable, while winters are mild but can bring occasional rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Virgen de la Peña Trail in Puebla de Guzmán. Access to natural trails and hermitages in this region is typically free.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the "Camino a la Virgen de la Peña" for approximately 32% and 6% of the route, indicating shared paths with other local routes leading to the hermitage.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly detailed, trails leading to hermitages, especially those described as a 'climb,' often reward hikers with panoramic views from their elevated positions. The presence of the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Peña suggests a prominent location with potential scenic overlooks.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months. Comfortable hiking shoes, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks are also advisable. Given the religious highlights, you might also consider bringing a camera.
